entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Short-duration variety of legume used in crop rotations to maintain soil fertility and increase profitability

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE FARMING
entities.description: Farming practices aimed at maintaining long-term productivity and environmental health

entities.name: IMPROVED TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: New agricultural methods or varieties adopted by farmers to improve efficiency and sustainability

entities.name: ON FARM SURVEY
entities.description: A formal data collection method used to gather information from farmers about their practices

entities.name: RAPID RURAL APPRAISALS
entities.description: A participatory research method used to quickly assess rural conditions and farmer perspectives

entities.name: DROUGHT PRONE AREA
entities.description: An environment characterized by frequent water scarcity, specifically in Central India

entities.name: CENTRAL INDIA
entities.description: The geographic region where the study on pigeonpea adoption was conducted

entities.name: SUGARCANE
entities.description: A cash crop cultivated in irrigated tracts that can lead to reduced yields if cultivated intensively

entities.name: COTTON
entities.description: A cash crop cultivated in irrigated tracts that can lead to reduced yields if cultivated intensively

entities.name: IRRIGATED TRACTS
entities.description: Areas of land provided with artificial water supply for the cultivation of cash crops

entities.name: REDUCED YIELDS
entities.description: A decrease in the amount of crop produced per unit area resulting from intensive cultivation

entities.name: INPUTS
entities.description: Resources such as fertilizers and pesticides whose use increases with intensive cultivation of cash crops

entities.name: CROP AND VARIETAL ADOPTION
entities.description: The process of farmers choosing and implementing appropriate crop varieties to maintain productivity

entities.name: MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Techniques implemented by farmers to maintain long-term productivity levels

entities.name: LONG-TERM PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The ability of a farming system to maintain output levels over an extended period

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth, maintained via crop rotations

entities.name: NITROGEN-FIXING LEGUMES
entities.description: Crops capable of converting atmospheric nitrogen into a form usable by plants to improve soil fertility

entities.name: CROP ROTATIONS
entities.description: The practice of growing different crops in succession on the same land to maintain soil fertility

entities.name: POST RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The period following the rainy season, allowing for the cultivation of a second crop

relationships.description: connection between farmers' concerns and adoption
